what_it_does: Stores YAML frontmatter metadata for an image attachment tied to a design-source channel record.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter and no body content.
It declares an attachment of type 'images'.
The target is a URI-like reference 'image://design-source/vt-residential/p-2'.
standouts: The target uses an 'image://' URI scheme pointing to 'design-source/vt-residential/p-2'.
conventions: Uses a YAML frontmatter block with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
References external media via a URI-style target rather than a filesystem path.
concerns: The image target URI provides no extension, dimensions, or MIME type.
There is no recorded author ('added_by') or title/alt text for the image.
It is unclear how the URI 'image://design-source/vt-residential/p-2' resolves to an actual asset.
notes: A minimal metadata stub that links a design-source channel entry to an image asset via a URI-style target.
questions: How is the 'image://' URI resolved to a concrete file?
What are the image's dimensions, format, and content?
purpose_vs_actual: The file's purpose as an attachment metadata record matches its actual content.
intention_signals: It appears intended to register an image asset as an attachment to a design-source channel record so the image can be located and presented in context.
novelty: The use of an 'image://' URI scheme for the target is a slightly distinctive addressing convention.
contribution: It contributes a timestamped, typed link between a channel record and an image asset, supporting media provenance and retrieval in a fused ledger.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML metadata attachment record of type 'images' linking a design-source channel entry to the target 'image://design-source/vt-residential/p-2' with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To register and reference an image asset associated with a design-source channel record.
